result.lens: Random / extremal graph theory outside the lineage: unit-distance graphs as sparse random CSP instances drawn from saturated arithmetic ring patches, with edge density dialed by the modulus-1 unit group of the coordinate ring.
result.first_principles:
Where does a unit-distance graph's edge mass come from? Fix a conjugation-closed subring R of C, dense in the plane. Its modulus-1 elements U = {u in R : |u| = 1} form a multiplicative group, and the unit-distance graph on R is the Cayley graph Cay((R,+), U), with automorphism group containing the semidirect product of R by U: every unit vector is simultaneously an edge direction and a rotation scalar. The degree of a saturated patch equals the number of realized elements of U, so edge density is manufactured by the rotation group. Read against the detectors, this is the R^1 check inverted: on the line the orthogonal group is {+-1}, so U = {+-1}, degree caps at 2, chi = 2. In the plane U grows without bound as we adjoin spindle units omega2 = (5 + i sqrt 11)/6 and half-angle composites mu = (omega1 - omega1^(-1))(omega2 - 1), with |mu| = 1 (verified exactly above).

Density alone is inert: Q^2's rings carry an index-2 additive subgroup whose nontrivial coset contains every rational unit vector (Woodall), so rational patches are bipartite at arbitrary degree. The Q^2 detector therefore forces an admissibility hypothesis: U must meet every small additive quotient nontrivially, which is exactly what sqrt 3 and sqrt 11 buy. Euclidean content enters twice more: two unit circles meet in at most 2 points, so codegree <= 2 and pair interactions are weak and local, like a sparse random CSP; in L-infinity codegree is a continuum and a 2-periodic quotient 4-coloring exists at every density, so no freezing can occur there.

A sparse CSP with q = 5 has a liquid phase (decorrelated, all pairs free), a clustered/frozen window, and an UNSAT threshold (Erdos-Renyi calibration: average degree near 12.8 and 13.7). The lineage sits at average degree near 10, deep in the liquid phase, and is SAT-minimized toward vertex-criticality, which (lemma, proved in Conjecture 2) excludes forced pairs outright. L57's 2.29 million free pairs are a phase diagnosis, not bad luck. The missing clamp lives at degree 12-16 in saturated ring patches, which are born realized: W3 never arises for them.

conjectures.name: Freezing-Window Conjecture (FWC) for spindle-ring patches
conjectures.statement: Model RP(h,k,rho): let omega1 = (1+i sqrt3)/2, omega2 = (5+i sqrt11)/6, mu = (omega1 - omega1^(-1))(omega2 - 1) (|mu| = 1, exact). Let S_h = {omega1^a omega2^b mu^c : 0 <= a <= 5, |b|+|c| <= h} (deduplicated), V(h,k) = all sums of at most k elements of S_h, and G(h,k,rho) the unit-distance graph induced on a Bernoulli(rho) subsample of V(h,k), with ALL exact unit pairs as edges (including accidental units not in S_h). Let d(rho) be the average degree. Claim: for (h,k) = (2,3), (i) LIQUID: for d <= 11, every non-adjacent pair is free with probability tending to 1 as the patch grows (matching the lineage at d near 10, retrodicting L57); (ii) WINDOW: there is a rho with d in [11.5, 16] where P(chi = 5) >= 1/2 and P(some non-adjacent pair is non-free | chi = 5) >= 0.05; additionally 4-colorability is lost at d in [9,12] (transplanted Erdos-Renyi 4-UNSAT threshold 8.9 plus O(1) geometric correction), so the model mass-produces out-of-lineage chi-5 UDGs.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Run the density sweep (experiment below). Refutations, in increasing severity: (a) random patches remain 4-colorable at d = 12 across 20+ samples (kills the threshold transplant at the q=4 level already); (b) zero non-free pairs found at any d up to 16 across 30+ chi-5 patches and 5000+ tested pairs, while patches remain 5-colorable (kills claim (ii)); (c) a non-free pair found at d <= 10 (kills claim (i) and, interestingly, contradicts the L57-consistent liquid picture).
conjectures.why_plausible: Quantitative retrodiction: the lineage's average degree is about 9.8-10.0, below the Erdos-Renyi q=5 clustering/condensation/UNSAT band 12.8-13.7 (Zdeborova-Krzakala), and L57 found exactly the all-free behavior the liquid phase predicts, exhaustively, on 2.29M pairs. Euclidean codegree <= 2 (two unit circles meet in <= 2 points) keeps pair interactions weak and CSP-like. UDG patches can reach d = 16 because the ring's unit group is infinite (Erdos's superconstant unit-distance lower bound is the extremal shadow of the same fact).
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: Geometric structure is not Erdos-Renyi: abundant 4-cycles (any two unit vectors commute additively), boundary degree inhomogeneity, and possible crystalline 5-colorings could shift or close the window. Worst case for the conjecture: the plane is 5-colorable (rival omega<=3 hypothesis), then patches stay 5-colorable at every density and (ii) is false; the sweep then harvests increasingly ordered 5-colorings, which is itself new evidence about the chi=5 world. Also, globally forced pairs may only appear in a measure-zero sliver at the exact 5-UNSAT edge rather than a positive-width window.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Transplanting the clustering/condensation/freezing phase diagram of random graph coloring onto exact-distance arithmetic ring patches, with the lineage's exhaustively verified freedom (L57) reinterpreted as a liquid-phase diagnosis, is unstated anywhere I know. Nearest prior art: Zdeborova-Krzakala 2007 (random graph coloring transitions; no geometry), McDiarmid-Muller (chromatic number of random geometric DISK graphs, not exact-distance), Polymath16 ring discussions (never density-dialed within one ring).

conjectures.name: Essential-Pair Lemma and the Minimization Self-Defeat prediction
conjectures.statement: LEMMA (proved here, elementary): let G be 5-chromatic and (s,t) a non-adjacent pair with chi(G - s) <= 4. Then (s,t) is free: taking a proper 4-coloring of G - s and giving s the fresh color 5 produces a 5-coloring with c(s) != c(t); re-coloring t to 5 as well (legal: s,t non-adjacent, all neighbors of s and t use colors 1-4) produces a 5-coloring with c(s) = c(t). Hence every forced pair, same or different, requires chi(G - s) >= 5 AND chi(G - t) >= 5. COROLLARY: vertex-critical chi-5 graphs contain no forced pairs of any kind; every clamp has at least 2 chi-redundant vertices (s and t themselves); SAT vertex-minimization deletes exactly the vertices capable of hosting forced pairs, so the lineage production process structurally guaranteed the L56/L57 outcome. PREDICTION-CONJECTURE: all 12 lineage graphs (S199 through 874) are vertex-critical, making L57's 2.29M-pair freedom a corollary of the lemma.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: For each lineage graph, SAT-test 4-colorability of G - v for every vertex v (early exit on first redundant vertex). Prediction: zero redundant vertices in all 12 graphs. Refuted by a single redundant vertex in any of them (the lemma still stands; only the total-retrodiction claim weakens, and L57's nontrivial content localizes to pairs of redundant vertices). Secondary check for ADVERSARY: search small vertex-critical chi-5 abstract graphs for a forced non-adjacent pair; finding one would mean my proof is wrong, since the lemma forbids it.
conjectures.why_plausible: The lemma is proved (two explicit colorings). Heule-style minimization loops terminate exactly when no vertex can be deleted while preserving 4-UNSAT, i.e., at vertex-criticality.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The lemma may be folklore in critical-graph theory (deflates novelty, not truth). Some stored lineage variants (e.g., partially minimized 1581-descendants) may carry redundant vertices, breaking the clean prediction. And the corollary only says where clamps are NOT: it constrains the search but produces nothing by itself.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The lemma is elementary and adjacent to Dirac/Stiebitz critical-graph folklore and Hajos-identification reasoning; I cannot rule out it appearing as an exercise somewhere. The program-level consequence is what I claim as new: SAT minimization provably removes every clamp-capable endpoint, so the de Grey/Polymath lineage's exhaustive pair-freedom (L57) was predetermined by its production process, and clamp search must optimize a different functional (redundant-core-preserving density, not vertex count). I have not seen this stated in the HN literature or Polymath16 threads.

conjectures.name: Ring-Saturation Conjecture (RSC)
conjectures.statement: Let R be the conjugation-closed ring generated by omega1, omega2, mu over Z[1/6], and S_h its height-h unit set as in FWC. Then chi(Cay((R,+), S_h)) is nondecreasing in h, and there is a finite saturation height h* (conjectured h* <= 3) with chi >= 6; moreover a truncated patch V(h*, k) with k <= 4 and fewer than 2x10^4 vertices is already 6-chromatic. Mechanism claim: the jump is carried by L55-route-(ii) wide-interface imprimitivity created by height-2 mu-composites (half-angle units), not by any single-vertex-port gadget (which the monoid obstruction forbids).
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: SAT 5-colorability of nested truncations of V(2,3), V(3,3), V(3,4) up to 2x10^4 vertices. Each returned 5-coloring falsifies that instance and pushes h*/witness-size upward; the conjecture as stated is refuted if every truncation with h <= 3, k <= 4, n <= 2x10^4 is 5-colorable. A single replicated 5-UNSAT (second solver + DRAT certificate) is chi(R^2) >= 6 outright, since these patches are realized by construction.
conjectures.why_plausible: de Grey's 1581 graph lives at low height inside this exact ring and already forces chi = 5 on a tiny, heavily pruned sub-patch; saturating instead of pruning monotonically adds constraints while admissibility (no parity-type quotient, irrational units) blocks the cheap periodic colorings; and map-type 6-colorings of the plane are impossible (Voronov), so if dense patches stay 5-colorable their colorings must be increasingly exotic, which is a tension that has to resolve one way or the other at finite height.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: If chi(R^2) = 5, no h* exists and every patch is 5-colorable forever. h* could exist with witness size far beyond SAT reach (the jump could need 10^6 vertices). Monotone density growth might not be monotone obstruction growth: L11/L14 warn that added rotations often color around; sumset saturation binds more than disjoint orbits, but possibly never enough.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Polymath16 studied chromatic numbers of FIXED small rings (Z[omega1, omega3] etc.) and candidate ring colorings. The saturation-height dial inside one fixed conjugation-closed ring, the monotonicity claim, the finite-jump-to-6 prediction with an explicit witness size bound, and the identification of the half-angle composite unit mu as the density carrier are, to my knowledge, unstated. Nearest art: Polymath16 algebraic threads; Exoo-Ismailescu field-based constructions.

conjectures.name: Marginal-Gap Law (no almost-frozen pairs in the liquid lineage)
conjectures.statement: For uniform-random proper 5-colorings of any lineage graph (e.g., P510), the pair marginal p(a,b) = P(c(a) = c(b)) over non-adjacent pairs concentrates in [0.1, 0.35], and NO pair has p < 0.02 or p > 0.6. Equivalently: L57's binary freedom is deep liquidity (near-independent marginals around 1/5), not the edge of near-forcing; there is no smooth gradient toward a clamp inside the lineage.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: Sample >= 200 diversified proper 5-colorings of P510 (randomized phases, value-branching seeds, blocking-clause chains, two solvers to control sampler bias), estimate p(a,b) on >= 2000 random non-adjacent pairs plus the full high-degree core. Refuted if any pair robustly shows p < 0.02 or p > 0.6 across both samplers. NOTE: refutation is GOOD for the program: an almost-frozen pair is a gradient, and the FWC generator would immediately target it by local densification around its endpoints.
conjectures.why_plausible: Liquid-phase decorrelation at average degree 10 predicts marginals near 1/5 with fluctuations; L18 showed the chi=5 obstruction is delocalized; L57's exhaustive zero-forcing is the p=0/p=1 endpoint statement of this law.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: SAT solution sampling is biased toward cluster cores; only order-of-magnitude tails should count as signal, and a fake tail could survive both samplers. The approximate D6 symmetry of the lineage (L15/L16) could create structured outlier pairs that look almost-frozen for symmetry reasons. If marginals are broad for benign entropic reasons, the gradient reading misleads.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Coloring-marginal statistics have never been computed on de Grey-lineage graphs; L45/L56/L57 are strictly binary (forced/free). Nearest art: belief-propagation marginal studies on random CSPs (Krzakala, Zdeborova et al.) and the uniquely-colorable-graph literature; neither touches unit-distance graphs.

experiment.description: One script, two stages, sharing infrastructure: c:\Users\Owen\dev\hadwiger-nelson\experiments\combinatorial\e8_ring_patch_sweep.py. STAGE 0 (decides Conjecture 2's prediction, runs first because it is cheapest): for each of the 12 lineage graphs, SAT-test 4-colorability of G - v for every v, early-exit per graph on first redundant vertex; output = vertex-criticality verdict per graph. If all 12 are vertex-critical, L57 is retrodicted by the Essential-Pair Lemma and the anti-minimization directive is proven for the lineage. STAGE 1 (decides FWC claims (i)-(ii) and probes RSC): (1) exact arithmetic: points as integer 5-tuples (a,b,c,d;m) representing (a + b sqrt3 + c sqrt11 + d sqrt33)/m with m | 6^j; squared modulus computed in pure integer arithmetic, sympy only to certify final edge lists of any patch that matters; (2) build S_h for h = 1,2 from {omega1^a omega2^b mu^c}, V(h,k) for k = 2,3 capped near 6000 points; (3) edges = numpy float prefilter (|dist^2 - 1| < 1e-6) then exact integer confirmation, so accidental units (out-of-lineage binding) are caught by construction; (4) Bernoulli dilution sweep scanning average degree d across [8,17], about 40 patches; per patch SAT-test 4-col and 5-col; (5) for chi=5 patches, run shotD-style pair tests (merge-then-5-UNSAT and add-edge-then-5-UNSAT) on 200 top-codegree plus 100 random non-adjacent pairs, PRE-FILTERED by the lemma (only pairs with chi(G-s) = chi(G-t) = 5 can force, so test chi(G-v) first; this typically eliminates most pairs for free); (6) outputs: phase table (d, P(4col), P(5col), count of non-free pairs), graphs as JSON + DIMACS under experiments/combinatorial/_cache/e8/. Decisive readouts: 4-UNSAT onset location (early falsification point for the threshold transplant), first-ever mass production of out-of-lineage chi-5 UDGs, onset or absence of non-free pairs through d = 16, and any 5-UNSAT patch (RSC jackpot, replicate immediately with a second solver).
experiment.repo_feasibility: Every component exists: exact Q(sqrt3, sqrt11) arithmetic patterns in experiments/combinatorial/e1d_field_extension_search.py and e1x_realize_moser14.py; the UDG interface in experiments/_shared/unit_distance_graph.py; pair-test code in experiments/combinatorial/shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py; python-sat (cadical) installed and proven on 2.29M solves (L57); no C compiler, no nauty, no new dependencies needed; patch sizes (<= 6000 vertices, <= 50k edges) are inside the routine SAT range for this host.
experiment.est_cost: Coding about half a day (mostly assembling existing pieces). Stage 0: 2-8 hours of SAT (about 7000 single-deletion solves, seconds each, early exits likely). Stage 1: 6-12 hours of SAT across the sweep. Total under 2 days wall clock.

detector_self_check.Q2: Density is explicitly conditioned on admissibility: the ring's unit group must meet every small-index additive quotient nontrivially. Q^2's rings fail this (Woodall's index-2 subgroup has all rational unit vectors in its nontrivial coset), so the framework predicts rational patches stay bipartite at ARBITRARY average degree, i.e., it predicts chi(Q^2) = 2, not >= 3. Q^2 is in fact the motivating counterexample that forced the admissibility hypothesis: the Euclidean content enters as irrational spindle units (sqrt3, sqrt11) that saturate the small quotients. Z^2 scaled by 1/sqrt(k) with k odd is the same trap (huge degree, bipartite) and is excluded the same way. The Essential-Pair Lemma is norm-agnostic but only proves where clamps are ABSENT; it proves no lower bound anywhere.
detector_self_check.Linf: Two load-bearing hypotheses are Euclidean and fail in L-infinity. First, the CSP analogy needs weak local pair interactions, justified by codegree <= 2 (two Euclidean unit circles meet in at most 2 points); L-infinity unit spheres share segments, giving continuum codegree and giant bicliques, so the sparse-CSP phase picture never applies. Second, L-infinity admits the 2-periodic square quotient 4-coloring at EVERY density, exactly the kind of quotient coloring that admissibility is designed to exclude; its patches are permanently liquid at q = 4. The framework therefore predicts no freezing window and no chi >= 5 in L-infinity, consistent with Chilakamarri's chi = 4. It does not over-prove there.
detector_self_check.R1: The entire density dial is an O(2) resource: the degree of a saturated patch equals the number of realized modulus-1 ring elements, i.e., the size of a finitely generated subgroup of the rotation group acting as ring automorphisms. On the line the orthogonal group is {+-1}, the unit set is {+-1} forever, maximum degree is 2, and patches are unions of paths with chi = 2. The construction cannot even be instantiated on R^1, so nothing in it constrains chi(R) = 2. The proposal is rotation-anchored, not rotation-blind: removing O(2) removes the generator.
